questions.rgb_answer: The visual clue that most directly supports identifying the scene as a Gothic cathedral facade is the presence of the rose window. This circular window with intricate tracery is a hallmark of Gothic architecture and is typically found in the façade of such buildings. Its position above the main entrance, flanked by two tall towers with flying buttresses, further reinforces the Gothic style.
